content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660            322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

October 31, 1983


Michael D. Antonovich, Chairman
Board of Supervisors
Los Angeles County
Room 869, Hall of Administration
500 West Temple Street
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re: Your Letter of July 26, 1983
     Our No. A-83-231

Dear Supervisor Antonovich:

     Roger Brown has referred your letter of July 26, 1983, to
the Legal Division of the Commission for our advice. Your
letter reflects that you have correctly valued the tickets which
you received from MCA. However, for your future information,
you may not reduce the value of a gift by subsequent payment if
that payment occurs after 30 days from the receipt of the gift.
In addition, in valuing any gift tickets received from such an
entity such as MCA, the Dodgers, etc., you must include the value of
any parking pass or parking privileges, souvenir programs, etc.,
received from the same donor.

     You should remember that any gift you receive which has a
value of $250 or more could form the basis for disqualification
for a period of 12 months from its receipt.1/ Consequently,
if the correct valuation of any of your gifts from MCA, the
Dodgers, etc. is $250 or more, then you may have to disqualify
yourself during the appropriate 12-month period.

_________________________________________________________________

1/ Government Code Section 82030(a) defines "income" as
including any "gift." Section 87103(c) states that a source of
income (gift) of $250 or more during the preceding 12 months
will be the basis for a disqualification if the other
requirements of Section 87103 are met.

Michael D. Antonovich, Chairman
October 31, 1983
Page 2


    Should you have any questions regarding this letter, I may
be reached at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Robert B. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:plh
cc  John H. Larson, County Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: What is the proper valuation of tickets received as gifts from MCA and the Dodgers, and does this trigger disqualification obligations under Government Code Section 87103?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that gifts of tickets must be valued to include all ancillary benefits (parking passes, programs, etc.), cannot be reduced in value by payments made more than 30 days after receipt, and if valued at $250 or more may require 12-month disqualification from participation in decisions affecting the gift source.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections in document header, but letter structure clearly identifies the legal issue and FPPC's advice. Response to incoming request letter dated July 26, 1983. Citations to Government Code Sections 82030(a) and 87103(c) provided in footnote.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the proper valuation of tickets received as gifts from MCA and the Dodgers, and does this trigger disqualification obligations under Government Code Section 87103?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that gifts of tickets must be valued to include all ancillary benefits (parking passes, programs, etc.), cannot be reduced in value by payments made more than 30 days after receipt, and if valued at $250 or more may require 12-month disqualification from participation in decisions affecting the gift source.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Supervisor Antonovich regarding valuation of tickets received as gifts and the potential disqualification obligations. The FPPC clarifies that the full value including ancillary benefits must be considered, late payments cannot reduce valuation, and gifts of $250+ may trigger 12-month disqualification.
